architecture_decisions.decision: Create internal/shared/ratelimit package with Redis-based sliding window algorithm
architecture_decisions.rationale: Centralized rate limiting logic using Redis for distributed state, sliding window provides smooth rate limiting without fixed window spikes

architecture_decisions.decision: Implement hierarchical policy resolution: Global > Endpoint > User > API Key
architecture_decisions.rationale: Allows flexible override system where more specific policies take precedence over general ones

architecture_decisions.decision: Use Redis sorted sets (ZSET) for sliding window implementation
architecture_decisions.rationale: O(log N) operations for add/remove, automatic expiration support, efficient range queries for window counting

architecture_decisions.decision: Separate rate limit metrics from enforcement logic
architecture_decisions.rationale: Enables dry-run mode for policy testing without blocking requests, supports observability

architecture_decisions.decision: Integrate with existing OpenTelemetry middleware for distributed tracing
architecture_decisions.rationale: Maintains consistency with existing observability stack, traces rate limit decisions

architecture_decisions.decision: Implement circuit breaker as separate concern from rate limiting
architecture_decisions.rationale: Different failure modes (rate limit vs service degradation), independent configuration needs

architecture_decisions.decision: Priority queue using Redis sorted lists with priority scores
architecture_decisions.rationale: Leverages existing Redis infrastructure, enables O(1) push/pop operations for queued requests

architecture_decisions.decision: Extend existing internal/multi-tenant/quota.go for print quota system
architecture_decisions.rationale: Reuses existing quota infrastructure, adds print-specific quota types (pages, color, jobs)

backend_tasks.purpose: Core sliding window rate limiter using Redis sorted sets

Burst vs sustained rate limit implementation

OpenTelemetry metrics for rate limiting
Violation alert notification system
Database repository for policy and violation storage
Database schema for rate limiting
Policy management API handlers
Violation log viewing API
Trusted client management API
Circuit breaker status and control API
Route registration for rate limit APIs
Print-specific quota system extending base quota
Add quota validation to job submission
Secure print release with card/PIN authentication
Cost allocation engine for print jobs
Integrate rate limit middleware into existing middleware chain

frontend_tasks.purpose: TypeScript types for rate limiting entities
API client for rate limit endpoints
Policy management page with CRUD operations
Secure print release interface with PIN/card entry
React hook for rate limit data fetching
WebSocket hook for real-time metrics updates

database_migrations.sql:
-- Rate limit policies table
CREATE TABLE rate_limit_policies (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    policy_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (policy_type IN ('global', 'endpoint', 'user', 'api_key', 'ip')),
    endpoint_pattern VARCHAR(500),
    limit_per_minute INTEGER DEFAULT 60,
    limit_per_hour INTEGER DEFAULT 1000,
    limit_per_day INTEGER DEFAULT 10000,
    burst_limit INTEGER DEFAULT 10,
    burst_window_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 10,
    require_auth BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    bypass_on_trusted BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    queue_when_exceeded BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    queue_max_wait_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 30,
    circuit_breaker_threshold INTEGER DEFAULT 100,
    circuit_breaker_window_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 60,
    circuit_breaker_open_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 300,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    UNIQUE(tenant_id, name)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_rate_limit_policies_tenant ON rate_limit_policies(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_rate_limit_policies_active ON rate_limit_policies(tenant_id, is_active);
CREATE INDEX idx_rate_limit_policies_type ON rate_limit_policies(tenant_id, policy_type);

-- Trusted clients table
CREATE TABLE trusted_clients (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    client_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (client_type IN ('api_key', 'ip_range', 'user_agent', 'service_account')),
    identifier VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(255),
    description TEXT,
    tier VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'standard' CHECK (tier IN ('free', 'standard', 'premium', 'enterprise')),
    bypass_rate_limit BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    custom_limits JSONB,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP,
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    UNIQUE(tenant_id, client_type, identifier)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_trusted_clients_tenant ON trusted_clients(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_trusted_clients_active ON trusted_clients(tenant_id, is_active);

-- Rate limit violations table
CREATE TABLE rate_limit_violations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    policy_id UUID REFERENCES rate_limit_policies(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    identifier_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    identifier_value VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    endpoint VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    method VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    limit_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    limit_value INTEGER NOT NULL,
    actual_value INTEGER NOT NULL,
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    ip_address INET,
    user_agent TEXT,
    blocked BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    queued BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    circuit_breaker_triggered BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    alert_sent BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    metadata JSONB,
    occurred_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_rate_limit_violations_tenant ON rate_limit_violations(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_rate_limit_violations_occurred ON rate_limit_violations(occurred_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_rate_limit_violations_identifier ON rate_limit_violations(tenant_id, identifier_type, identifier_value);
CREATE INDEX idx_rate_limit_violations_policy ON rate_limit_violations(policy_id);

-- Circuit breaker states table
CREATE TABLE circuit_breaker_states (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),
    service_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    endpoint VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    state VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (state IN ('closed', 'open', 'half_open')),
    failure_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    last_failure_time TIMESTAMP,
    last_state_change TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    threshold INTEGER DEFAULT 100,
    window_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 60,
    open_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 300,
    half_open_max_calls INTEGER DEFAULT 3,
    metadata JSONB,
    UNIQUE(tenant_id, service_name, endpoint)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_circuit_breaker_states_tenant ON circuit_breaker_states(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_circuit_breaker_states_state ON circuit_breaker_states(state);

-- Print quotas table (market-driven feature)
CREATE TABLE print_quotas (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    cost_center_id UUID,
    quota_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (quota_type IN ('pages', 'jobs', 'color_pages', 'duplex_pages', 'large_format')),
    period_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (period_type IN ('daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'quarterly', 'yearly')),
    limit INTEGER NOT NULL,
    used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    soft_limit INTEGER,
    period_start DATE NOT NULL,
    period_end DATE NOT NULL,
    rollover_unused BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    allow_overdraft BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    overdraft_limit INTEGER,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(tenant_id, user_id, quota_type, period_type, period_start)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_print_quotas_tenant ON print_quotas(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_print_quotas_user ON print_quotas(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_print_quotas_cost_center ON print_quotas(cost_center_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_print_quotas_period ON print_quotas(period_start, period_end);

-- Cost allocation rules table (market-driven feature)
CREATE TABLE cost_allocation_rules (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
    allocation_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (allocation_type IN ('user', 'department', 'cost_center', 'project', 'client')),
    match_criteria JSONB NOT NULL,
    cost_per_page_monochrome DECIMAL(10,4),
    cost_per_page_color DECIMAL(10,4),
    cost_per_job DECIMAL(10,4),
    cost_per_mb DECIMAL(10,4),
    apply_to_users JSONB,
    apply_to_printers JSONB,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(tenant_id, name)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_cost_allocation_rules_tenant ON cost_allocation_rules(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_cost_allocation_rules_active ON cost_allocation_rules(tenant_id, is_active);

-- Secure print release table (market-driven feature)
CREATE TABLE secure_print_releases (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    job_id UUID NOT NULL,
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    release_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL CHECK (release_type IN ('card', 'pin', 'biometric', 'mobile')),
    release_token VARCHAR(255),
    card_id VARCHAR(100),
    pin_hash VARCHAR(255),
    device_id VARCHAR(100),
    released_at TIMESTAMP,
    expires_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'released', 'expired', 'cancelled')),
    ip_address INET,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_secure_print_releases_tenant ON secure_print_releases(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_secure_print_releases_job ON secure_print_releases(job_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_secure_print_releases_user ON secure_print_releases(user_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_secure_print_releases_status ON secure_print_releases(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_secure_print_releases_expires ON secure_print_releases(expires_at);
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS secure_print_releases, cost_allocation_rules, print_quotas, circuit_breaker_states, rate_limit_violations, trusted_clients, rate_limit_policies CASCADE;

e2e_tests.name: RateLimitEnforcement
steps: Create rate limit policy with 10 requests per minute

e2e_tests.name: TrustedClientBypass
steps: Add client IP to trusted list
Send requests exceeding limit from trusted IP

e2e_tests.name: BurstRateLimiting
steps: Create policy with burst of 5, sustained rate of 1 per second

e2e_tests.name: CircuitBreakerIntegration
steps: Set circuit breaker threshold to 5 failures

e2e_tests.name: PriorityQueueIntegration
steps: Configure queue with max wait 5 seconds
Send request exceeding rate limit with priority=high
Process queue, high priority request handled first

e2e_tests.name: PrintQuotaEnforcement
steps: Create user with 100 page monthly quota

e2e_tests.name: SecurePrintRelease
steps: Submit print job with secure release
Job queued but not sent to printer
Send release request with valid token

e2e_tests.name: CostAllocation
steps: Create cost allocation rule for Engineering department
Submit print job from engineering user
Cost calculated based on page count and color

security_notes: Rate limit bypass tokens must be cryptographically signed and rotated regularly
Trusted IP ranges should be validated and stored in CIDR notation to prevent IP spoofing bypasses
PIN hashes for secure print release must use bcrypt with minimum work factor 12
Rate limit metadata should be sanitized to prevent injection attacks
Circuit breaker state changes must be logged for security audit
API keys for trusted clients must have expiration dates and rotation policies
Cost allocation rules should enforce tenant isolation to prevent cross-tenant cost leakage
Print quota operations must be idempotent to prevent double-spending
Secure release tokens must be single-use and expire within configurable timeout (default 24 hours)
Rate limit headers should not leak internal system details or configuration
Violation alert payloads must not contain sensitive user data in clear text
Redis connections for rate limiting must use TLS in production environments
docker_changes: Add RATELIMIT_ENABLED environment variable to all service containers
Add RATELIMIT_REDIS_ADDRESS and RATELIMIT_REDIS_PASSWORD environment variables
Add secure-print-release container for card/PIN validation service
Update health check endpoints to include rate limit status
Add prometheus rate limit metrics exporter sidecar container
market_driven_features: Print Quota System (FR-M013) - PaperCut competitor feature with per-user/per-department page limits, configurable periods, and soft/hard limit enforcement
Secure Print Release (FR-M014) - Card/PIN/biometric authentication for job release, matching PaperCut's secure release functionality
Cost Allocation Engine (FR-M015) - Per-page cost tracking by user/department/project, competitive with YSoft SafeQ's reporting
Mobile Print Apps (FR-M016) - Rate limit API includes mobile app detection and appropriate throttling for mobile networks
Advanced Analytics Dashboard (FR-M020) - Real-time rate limit metrics visualization rivaling competitor offerings
